# frozen_string_literal: true

module Wrappers
  module Smarty
    # API methods
    module Api
      ENDPOINTS = {
        autocomplete: "us-autocomplete-pro-cloud",
        verify: "us-core-cloud"
      }.freeze

      def autocomplete(query:, ip_address:, selected: nil)
        client = SmartyStreets::ClientBuilder.new(@configuration)
                                             .with_custom_headers({ "X-Forwarded-For": ip_address })
                                             .with_licenses([ENDPOINTS[__method__]])
                                             .build_us_autocomplete_pro_api_client

        lookup = SmartyStreets::USAutocompletePro::Lookup.new(query)
        lookup.selected = selected if selected.present?
        client.send(lookup)
      end

      def verify(opts)
        ip_address = opts.delete(:ip_address).to_s
        client = SmartyStreets::ClientBuilder.new(@configuration)
                                             .with_custom_headers({ "X-Forwarded-For": ip_address })
                                             .with_licenses([ENDPOINTS[__method__]])
                                             .build_us_street_api_client

        lookup = SmartyStreets::USStreet::Lookup.new

        opts.each { |k, v| lookup.send("#{k}=", v) }
        client.send_lookup(lookup)
      end
    end
  end
end

{"output":"/Users/duhl/.profile: line 1: /Users/duhl/.cargo/env: No such file or directory

# frozen_string_literal: true

module Api
  module Public
    module V3
      # v3 api endpoint for smarty proxy
      class SmartyController < BaseController
        AUTH_ID = ENV.fetch("SMARTY_AUTH_ID")
        AUTH_TOKEN = ENV.fetch("SMARTY_SECRET_KEY")

        skip_before_action :set_action_page
        skip_before_action :set_account

        def autocomplete
          json = client.autocomplete(
            query: params[:q],
            selected: params[:selected],
            ip_address: request.remote_ip
          )
          render json:, status: :ok
        end

        def verify
          json = client.verify(verify_params.to_h)
          render json:, status: :ok
        end

      private

        def client
          Wrappers::Smarty::Client.new(auth_id: AUTH_ID, auth_token: AUTH_TOKEN)
        end

        VALID_VERIFY_PARAMS = %i[
          street
          street2
          secondary
          city
          state
          zipcode
          lastline
          match
        ].freeze

        def verify_params
          params.slice(*VALID_VERIFY_PARAMS).permit!
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

payload.text:
**Summarizing Smarty API parameter handling**

I'm explaining how the controller whitelists and processes parameters for the Smarty API, referencing specific files like `app/controllers/api/public/v3/smarty_controller.rb:24` and `lib/wrappers/smarty/api.rb`. I confirm that the parameters sent (city, state, street, zipcode, etc.) align with the allowed list and how the `verify` method builds the lookup object by assigning these. I’ll note that `ip_address` is optional and only used in headers, not as a query parameter. The final message will clarify this with bullet points referencing relevant lines and parameter treatment.

payload.message:
- `app/controllers/api/public/v3/smarty_controller.rb:24` slices the incoming query down to `street`, `street2`, `secondary`, `city`, `state`, `zipcode`, `lastline`, and `match`, then passes that hash straight through to `Wrappers::Smarty::Client#verify`. No other fields are sent with the request.
- In `lib/wrappers/smarty/api.rb:23`, `verify` removes any `ip_address`, builds a `SmartyStreets::USStreet::Lookup`, and then does `lookup.send("#{k}=", v)` for each entry that came from the controller. The SDK’s lookup class (`vendor/cache/smartystreets_ruby_sdk-6.2.0/lib/smartystreets_ruby_sdk/us_street/lookup.rb:10`) defines accessors for exactly those attributes, so each permitted param becomes the corresponding field in the Smarty request body.
- Given your query (`street=28+Cherry+Dr&city=Bozem&state=MT&zipcode=59715`), every key matches one of the permitted/forwarded fields, so they will be populated on the lookup object the SDK sends to the Smarty “us-core-cloud” endpoint. If you need additional inputs (e.g., `secondary`, `match`), they must be included in the whitelist before they’ll be forwarded.

content.text:
- `app/controllers/api/public/v3/smarty_controller.rb:24` slices the incoming query down to `street`, `street2`, `secondary`, `city`, `state`, `zipcode`, `lastline`, and `match`, then passes that hash straight through to `Wrappers::Smarty::Client#verify`. No other fields are sent with the request.
- In `lib/wrappers/smarty/api.rb:23`, `verify` removes any `ip_address`, builds a `SmartyStreets::USStreet::Lookup`, and then does `lookup.send("#{k}=", v)` for each entry that came from the controller. The SDK’s lookup class (`vendor/cache/smartystreets_ruby_sdk-6.2.0/lib/smartystreets_ruby_sdk/us_street/lookup.rb:10`) defines accessors for exactly those attributes, so each permitted param becomes the corresponding field in the Smarty request body.
- Given your query (`street=28+Cherry+Dr&city=Bozem&state=MT&zipcode=59715`), every key matches one of the permitted/forwarded fields, so they will be populated on the lookup object the SDK sends to the Smarty “us-core-cloud” endpoint. If you need additional inputs (e.g., `secondary`, `match`), they must be included in the whitelist before they’ll be forwarded.
